concerns.text: Task T014 implements a '[deferred]' threshold for missing data warnings, but spec.md Edge Cases explicitly define a concrete acceptance criterion: 'If missing data is ≤10%, the system proceeds without warning.' The task silently relaxes this deterministic rule by deferring the threshold, violating the spec's acceptance criteria.

Task T028 allows degradation to OLS if N > 500k, but spec.md FR-005 mandates fitting 'at least three distinct spatial models' (OLS, GWR, SAR) without specifying a degradation path. The task introduces a fallback that effectively drops the GWR/SAR requirement under common conditions, weakening the functional requirement.

Task T032 implements 'Proxy Validity Sensitivity' (FR-010) but the spec.md Functional Requirements list ends at FR-009. FR-010 exists only in plan.md. The task implements a requirement not present in the spec, creating a scope drift where the deliverable satisfies a plan constraint but not the ratified spec.

Task T034 implements the GWR bandwidth sweep (FR-009) in a separate 'Phase 6' rather than integrating it into the modeling validation flow. Spec.md FR-009 and SC-004 imply this is part of the model validation. Separating it risks disjointed execution and failure to link results to the main model output as required by SC-004.

Task T015 generates metadata.json but omits the explicit task to write 'data-model.md' required by plan.md Constitution Check VI ('Spatial Resolution Integrity'). The spec requires documenting reprojection/resampling methods; the task list fails to include the artifact generation for this constitutional requirement.
